item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article cannot be conclusively sourced, and no source found indicates it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A nominal downvote. Nothing against it, but it's just weak. I'm not sure what might strengthen it, but, well … meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for the whimsy and breath of fresh air.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: upvoted, and i'd do it again if there was a test log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This thing is so freaking meh, I can't even bear to give it a novote. Sorry to the writer, but this one is a downvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is incredibly charming. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea and it has potential, but there's a significant lack of a hook.

No vote for now, it's okay but could use improvements.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually kinda like this one, in that it doesn't do much but I think what it does is amusing. My problem with the article is really just the way that it's written - I kept hitting phrases that struck me as "off" in one way or another.

due to the fragile nature of the commonly manufactured balloons which it resembles

For example, I feel like this is wordier than it needs to be? Or maybe it's just a little cumbersome.

While it does exhibit the buoyancy expected from its helium content, the object's movements seem to be unaffected by air current and lift.

The way this is written implies that "the object's movements" is the subject of the first clause as well as the second, when in fact the subject of the first clause is supposed to be "the object," so I think there's a bit of a grammatical issue there.

As the effect of realizing there is extraneous audio is somewhat disquieting, a targeted group noticing the effect will oftentimes inadvertently end the object's effects.

Again, I feel like this is a very awkward way to say that the object creeps people out and ruins the party. I like the idea that the balloon just wants to hang out at a party but just makes things weird and unsettling, but I feel like there could be another way to incorporate that into the article? It sounds tacked-on as it is now.

Observed targeted groups have engaged in binge drinking games, food fights, dancing without music, physical children’s games, and orgies among other activities.

…I'm not sure 'orgies' is really a technical term. 'Group sexual activity' or some variation thereof might be better for the tone.

For now, I'm not downvoting this article, but the writing is keeping me from upvoting, either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a feeling of unexploited potential, and the lack distracted me from enjoying the article.

I like the implications that the balloon doesn't care about how people are having a good time, but then you don't go anywhere with it. Take the chanting sounds; think of the creepiness of a child's bauble just randomly floating around a human sacrifice, subtly encouraging the cultists to have a really good time with the vicious mutilations. Then floating off to the Chuckie Cheese a few blocks over and helping make a succesful birthday party for little Timmy.

But none of that is really hit upon, and it's a shame. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The "breath of fresh air", best consumed as a sorbet between xk tales. Good on ya.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a balloon that makes a party!
…
…
…
So yeah. 's about it.
I'm just gonna… yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Moans indicative of sexual activity.

And then I downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this one. It's anomalous, it's fairly subtle, and honestly it creeps me out without doing a single thing that's even slightly malevolent. A possibly-sentient balloon that doesn't drift in the breeze but can move on its own? I don't care if it wants to cheer me up, it's still creepy. Its simple existence would mean we definitely don't fully understand the universe yet and I imagine the Foundation would want to learn what it could from Mr. Happy Fun-times Balloon.

+1 for a simple and effective item.

One quick nit-pick: "humanoid voices" doesn't make sense. While the suffix "-oid" can be translated as "-like," thus making humanoid mean "human-like," this word is referring specifically to form or shape. Because a voice doesn't take a shape, you can't have a human-shaped voice.

item.title: Re: New Addition
item.content:encoded:
the ritualistic murder bit has been eliminated

I'm going out on a limb here and guessing this was removed after my vote and post?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm confused as to where I contradicted myself, CastChaos. Could you quote the conflicting sentences, if you have a chance?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I''m not going to say my initial foray into SCP writing is fantastic or anything, but I feel your "could be said quicker" test could be equally applied to a great many well regarded articles.

"Item is an inanimate concrete and steel statue, vaguely humanoid in shape and crudely painted. When unobserved by sentient organisms, it animates and attempts to terminate any creatures it can perceive with incredible strength and force. Any sentient observation of the statue immediately ends this effect."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Same here. I would love an addition like that, but I'll upvote anyway.

(Also because the false equations of "safe" with "anomalous" should be countered.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gotta agree with the others that this reads more like an Anomalous Item than a SCP:

"Item is a red, helium-filled balloon. When untethered, it will drift towards the closest group of at least 4 people, who are in a "happy mood". Once within 4.5 meters of the group, each individual will gradually exhibit more manic behaviors, apparently due to increased mood. This includes an increased willingness to engage in a wide array of pleasurable activites, regardless of outside stimuli or appropriateness. The effects will continue for a maximum of 2 hours after initial exposure to the balloon, or if the group within range drops below 4 people."

There. Total effects of the balloon summarized into a short paragraph.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, this is a balloon that deliberately floats to groups of people and gives party sounds, with "party" interpreted in a very wide meaning?

Yes, this is rather just anomalous, why waste such a long article on it? And the last sentence that says that people will be playing drinking games or having sex orgies contradicts the other parts of the last paragraph that claims that the balloon doesn't affect the groups…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the honest feedback, Lumancer. Any way I could get you to elaborate on the tonal issue? I am quite interested in improvement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually had the exact same thought!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept shows some promise, but the writing is really rough, including but not limited to tone issues. Downvote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At approximately 2 hours of exposure, there seems to be no further effect. The targeted gathering seems to be under no compulsion to continue their activities, and behave with normal discretion in regards to commitments, exhaustion, and the like.

Hmm, I like this change enough to neutralize my downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually like this one—I imagine it "sighs" when it is ignored, and then just kind of drifts off…

item.title: Re: New Addition
item.content:encoded: Hmmm… while I don't know that one documented (and preventable by Foundation staff should they have so chose) death makes it a "crazies you to death" SCP, the ritualistic murder bit has been eliminated.

item.title: Re: New Addition
item.content:encoded: But…but…now it just crazies you to death…

item.title: Re: New Addition
item.content:encoded:
Noooooo >.< the end of that addition is worse, it is crazying-to-death! Let me try to give some examples of what I mean by "needs more", because your goal of a Safe SCP that doesn't hurt anyone is just fine.

Here are some of my favorites: The Concrete Man (simple, short, safe, almost poetic); The Magic Tothbrush (oldie but, imo, a goodie); The Tickle Monster (probably controversial, but in the vein of your concept, so be wary); aaaand because I only write Safes and this fits the bill, Rubber Ducky, which is a really frankly whimsical item with substantial tests that make the effect seem more weird/dangerous than it really is.

item.title: New Addition
item.content:encoded: To address concerns that the SCP didn't "do enough", I have included an additional hook I originally considered including, but axed. For those of you who have already taken a look I would humbly request a reread. The new addition is an additional paragraph at the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not dangerous. Never said it wasn't dangerous enough to be considered an scp. There simply isn't much in it. it's so simple you could write a few sentences about it and then you're done. It doesn't have to be dangerous, but kind you it has to interest the reader, usually that involves something called a "hook", something that "hooks" the reader and makes the article memorable, whether cringe worthy body horror or a compete. Mindfuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What are you implying about the author's sexual proclivities? ;)

As for the "anomalous object" aspect. I think I may need clarification on the difference. Must the object need to be dangerous if misused to be considered an SCP?

If so… I may want to tweak in another property I had originally brewed up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I thought the moving around was sort of creepy, maybe. The noises thing didn't seem creepy at all, just odd. I fully expected it to have other effects that would be more creepy. I can understand trying to write an SCP that doesn't inherently harm humans, since that is more than a little common, but I think you ended up just making it boring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally it reminded me of a tv show I once saw that had an episodethat went into balloon fetishes.

I will echo statements that it is fairly boring and more like an anomalous object than a proper scp though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A test log could be an interesting endeavor. Thanks for the suggestion!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the observations! Edits made to address some of these concerns.

Being my initial attempt, I decided to strive for something that does not attempt to hurt, control, or otherwise endanger human beings, but instead something that creates a slowly realized creepy effect. Could you clarify what you mean by "didn't do anything"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I figured— I actually thought that was amusing.

But this could have been written in just a few lines and fit in the Anomalous Items List, and that's my subjective test for most Safe items, unless they have a particularly interesting test log, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, different people have varying ideas of what constitutes a "party". It is attracted to any gathering where people have a positive attitude, regardless of context.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personnel should be cautious when handling the object due to its possibly fragile nature.

This sounds off to me for some reason.

SCP-1367 is a 45.7 cm helium filled balloon of common make and red coloration, tied off at the end by a 73.6 cm white string. The object has no identifying marks that would indicate a manufacturer.

45.7cm what? Diameter? Length of the string? "Tied off at the end" is bad tone.

the object's movements seem to be only mostly guided by air current and lift.

I cringed at that.

Besides those and possibly some other tone issues I've missed, this thing bores me. You had me interested with it following groups of happy people around, and with the noises thing, but then it didn't do anything. Not a single thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Moans indicative of sexual activity.
Chanting in an unknown language

Because when I think of party balloons, I think of sex and cult gatherings.

This really strikes me more as an Anomalous Item. There isn't enough to it to merit experimentation or containment procedures.
